{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/gdal/issues/8647","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/gdal","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/gdal/issues/8647/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/gdal/issues/8647/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/gdal/issues/8647/events","html_url":"https://github.com/OSGeo/gdal/issues/8647","id":1973882149,"node_id":"I_kwDOAF3Q3c51pw0l","number":8647,"title":"No way of getting the layer alias name in GDB files","user":{"login":"TheWeirdDev","id":6321734,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYzMjE3MzQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/6321734?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/TheWeirdDev","html_url":"https://github.com/TheWeirdDev","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/TheWeirdDev/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/TheWeirdDev/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/TheWeirdDev/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/TheWeirdDev/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/TheWeirdDev/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/TheWeirdDev/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/TheWeirdDev/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/TheWeirdDev/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/TheWeirdDev/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-11-02T09:51:15Z","updated_at":"2023-11-02T09:51:15Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"## Expected behavior and actual behavior.

Being able to retrieve the layer alias name of a layer from a GDB file.


I know it is possible to get the field alias names by using `GetAlternativeName`, but I'm talking about the layer itself and not the fields.

While reading the documentation I came across the [LAYER_ALIAS](https://gdal.org/drivers/vector/openfilegdb.html#layer-creation-options) option that allows setting the layer alias when creating the layer, but there is no information on how to retrieve this alias name when opening the file later (this also applies to the files created by ArcGIS)

## Steps to reproduce the problem.

Open a gdb file:

```python
from osgeo import ogr

driver = ogr.GetDriverByName('OpenFileGDB')
ds: ogr.DataSource = driver.Open('./TheFile.gdb')
layer: ogr.Layer = ds.GetLayerByName("TheLayer")
```
## What I tried

- `GetLayerDefn()`: I did not find anything related to the alias name or alternative name
- `GetMetadata()`: Returns an empty dict
- `GetDescription()`: Returns the layer name the same as `GetName()`, not the alias name

## What workaround I am currently using

I'm currently reading the `GDB_Items` table and parsing the xml content of its `Definition` column to find the `<AliasName>` tag.

## Operating system

Arch Linux (Rolling)
Debian (Bookworm)

## GDAL version and provenance

3.7.2 on Arch Linux from the official repo
3.6.2 on Debian from the bookworm stable repo","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/gdal/issues/8647/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/gdal/issues/8647/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}
